questions.text: What was Pharaoh's policy towards the Children of Israel at the time of Musa's birth?
questions.answer: He divided the people into sects, weakening one group by slaughtering their sons and letting their women live.

questions.text: What did Allah inspire the mother of Musa (AS) to do when she feared for him?
questions.answer: To nurse him, and then cast him into the river (Nile) in a chest.

questions.text: Who picked Musa (AS) up from the river?
questions.answer: The family of Pharaoh.

questions.text: What did the wife of Pharaoh (Asiya) say when they found the infant Musa?
questions.answer: 'A comfort to the eye for me and for you. Do not kill him; perhaps he may benefit us, or we may adopt him as a son'.

questions.text: How was Musa (AS) returned to his mother to be nursed?
questions.answer: Allah caused him to refuse all wet nurses until his sister suggested their mother, and he accepted.

questions.text: Why did Musa (AS) have to flee Egypt?
questions.answer: He accidentally killed an Egyptian while defending an Israelite and found out that the authorities were plotting to kill him.

questions.text: What act of kindness did Musa (AS) do when he reached the well of Madyan?
questions.answer: He watered the flocks for two women who were waiting because they couldn't compete with the male shepherds.

questions.text: What did one of the daughters of the old man of Madyan suggest regarding Musa?
questions.answer: She suggested her father hire him because 'the best one you can hire is the strong and the trustworthy'.

questions.text: What was the marriage contract between Musa (AS) and the father of the two women?
questions.answer: Musa would work for him for eight years, and if he completed ten, it would be from his own favor.

questions.text: Where did Musa (AS) see the fire and receive his first revelation after leaving Madyan?
questions.answer: On the side of Mount Tur.

questions.text: What was Haman's role in Pharaoh's court as mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: Pharaoh commanded him to build a high tower so Pharaoh could look at the God of Musa.

questions.text: What does Verse 56 say about the Prophet's ability to guide people?
questions.answer: 'Indeed, you do not guide whom you like, but Allah guides whom He wills'.

questions.text: Who was Qarun and what was his sin?
questions.answer: He was from the people of Musa but tyrannized them with his immense wealth, attributing his success to his own knowledge rather than Allah's favor.

questions.text: How massive was the wealth of Qarun described to be?
questions.answer: The keys to his treasure houses were a burden for a company of strong men to carry.

questions.text: What was the end of Qarun?
questions.answer: Allah caused the earth to swallow him and his home.

questions.text: To whom does Allah grant the 'Home of the Hereafter' according to Verse 83?
questions.answer: To those who do not desire exaltedness (arrogance) upon the earth or corruption.

questions.text: What is the 'Good Word' mentioned in Verse 84?
questions.answer: Whoever comes with a good deed will have better than it; whoever comes with an evil deed will only be recompensed for what they did.

questions.text: What promise did Allah give the Prophet (SAW) regarding his return to Makkah (Verse 85)?
questions.answer: That He who imposed the Quran upon him would surely take him back to a place of return (Makkah).

questions.text: How many years did Musa (AS) stay in Madyan before returning to Egypt?
questions.answer: At least ten years (the completed term of his contract).

questions.text: What is the final command in the Surah regarding other gods?
questions.answer: 'And do not invoke with Allah another deity. There is no deity except Him. Everything will be destroyed except His Face'.
